scratchpads.name: Nexus
scratchpads.content:
# Nexus Client GUI Audit (v3.0-alpha5)

Audited 4219 lines across 27 .jsx/.js/.css files.

## Dead Code (delete or wire up — pick one)
- **Scratchpad.jsx** (284 lines) — fully functional, NOT in App.jsx, SearchModal links to it but nav fails silently
- **Ledger.jsx** (140 lines) — fully functional, NOT in App.jsx, merged into Graph but file remains
- **UsageGauge.jsx** (102 lines) — explicitly removed from Sidebar, orphaned
- **ActivityHeatmap.jsx** (162 lines) — never imported anywhere

Decision: delete UsageGauge + ActivityHeatmap (replaced by Fuel module).
For Scratchpad/Ledger: either re-add as hidden tabs or delete the .jsx files.

## CLI/UI Asymmetry — 9 server features have ZERO client UI
1. /api/thoughts (Thought Stack)
2. /api/critique (Self-Critique)
3. /api/guard (Decision Guard)
4. /api/advice (Advice Journal)
5. /api/predict (Predictive Tasks)
6. /api/plan (Autonomous Plan)
7. /api/auto-summary (Overseer auto-writes)
8. /api/impact/forecast (AI migration assessments)

The CLI has 54 commands. The dashboard exposes maybe 20% of them.

## Critical Bugs
- **App.jsx**: SearchModal navigates to scratchpad module that does not exist — silent failure
- **Sidebar.jsx**: ICON_MAP missing terminal entry — Terminal nav item shows blank icon
- **No ErrorBoundary**: any module crash takes down the whole app
- **MissionBoard.jsx**: drag-and-drop visual feedback never wires through onDragStart prop
- **Fuel.jsx**: doesn't destructure ws in signature, breaking real-time updates

## Cross-Cutting Debt
- **Inconsistent fetch patterns**: 6+ components use raw fetch() instead of api.* helpers
- **useApi.js**: missing 15+ endpoints (no thoughts, critique, guard, advice, predict, plan, auto-summary, forecast, heatmap, clock, digest)
- **WebSocket under-used**: Pulse polls every 10s, Fuel every 30s, ClockWidget every 30s — should subscribe to push events
- **Terminal.jsx** creates its OWN WebSocket instead of using useWebSocket hook
- **No error toasts**: failed API calls fail silently across all modules
- **No loading skeletons**: full-screen compass spinner is jarring

## Mobile / Responsive
- **Sidebar.jsx**: w-56 fixed, no hamburger toggle on <768px (eats 30% of mobile width)
- **Fuel.jsx**: hardcoded grid-cols-2, never collapses
- **Pulse.jsx**: lg:grid-cols-4 breaks badly at 1280px with sidebar
- **MissionBoard.jsx**: drag-and-drop doesn't work on touch devices, no fallback

## Accessibility Gaps
- Compass spinners have no aria-label (3+ places)
- Color-only status indicators (gauges) — no patterns for colorblind
- Modal focus traps don't restore to trigger button
- No skip links in Sidebar
- Sessions expand button has no aria-expanded

## Top 5 Fixes (do these first)
1. **Wire missing modules + delete dead code** — 30 min — unblocks Scratchpad/Ledger or removes them cleanly
2. **Consolidate fetch into useApi.js** — 45 min — fixes silent failures + adds 15 missing endpoints
3. **Replace polling with WebSocket subscriptions** — 60 min — Pulse/Fuel/ClockWidget all should push
4. **Mobile responsiveness** — 40 min — hamburger sidebar + responsive grids
5. **ErrorBoundary + toast on failures** — 50 min — eliminates silent crashes

## Top 5 Enhancements (after fixes)
1. **Build Thought Stack module** — 90 min — first new feature with no UI
2. **Add filter/sort to ActivityStream + Sessions** — 45 min — currently flat lists are unusable past 100 entries
3. **Wire Decision Guard into MissionBoard** — 60 min — warn on duplicate task creation in the form
4. **Keyboard shortcut matrix** — 30 min — Ctrl+S, Ctrl+D, Ctrl+/ help modal
5. **Add auto-summary + forecast UI to Sessions and Graph** — 40 min — closes the wishlist gaps

Startup initialization sequence with animated 7-point health check (system, database, GPU, AI, git, projects, usage) that runs real diagnostics against all subsystems. Auto-remediation API with 7 whitelisted safe commands (git-status, git-stash, git-diff-stat, git-log, git-fetch, nexus-task-done, nexus-log) executable by the Overseer. Interpolates fuel between reports, calculates burn rates from weighted history, predicts runway (constrained by session/weekly/window), sizes workload into task categories (tiny/small/medium/large/huge). sessions.summary: v1.3 The Ledger shipped. Strategic decision index with 59 entries auto-extracted from session history. Schema: decision, context, project, alternatives, tags. API: /api/ledger (CRUD), /api/ledger/extract (auto-parse sessions). CLI: nexus record (manual), nexus decisions (view). Dashboard module with project grouping, expandable cards, tag filtering. Decisions also indexed in search. 10 dashboard modules, 32 CLI commands.

End of day 1. Nexus v1.3 stable on GitHub. 10 modules, 32 CLI commands, 59 ledger decisions, semantic search, Gemma 4 Overseer, smart fuel estimator. Built 13 versions in one day (v0.1-v1.3). Fleet: 5 active projects, 4 archived. The Overseer directed its own development from v0.7. The Cartographer remembers.

v1.3.1: Ledger integration + first tests. Brief now shows recent Ledger decisions per project. Overseer context includes Ledger (references past decisions when analyzing). First Vitest test suite: 10 tests covering usage tracking (CRUD, sorting, limits), task creation (auto-IDs, defaults), and Ledger (decisions with context/alternatives/projects). All green in 151ms.

v1.4 Hybrid Smart Search. Reciprocal Rank Fusion merges keyword + semantic results. Type-boosted: decisions 1.5x, sessions 1.3x, activity 0.7x. CLI: nexus search. Dot product for speed. Shared embedding cache.

v1.5 Knowledge Graph shipped. Decisions now form a traversable graph with bidirectional edges. 5 relationship types: led_to, replaced, depends_on, contradicts, related. Auto-linker found 129 connections from 59 decisions using temporal chains (sequential in same project), keyword overlap (shared terms), and tag matching (cross-project). Zero orphan nodes. CLI: nexus link (manual), nexus graph (stats + traverse), auto-link API. Store: addEdge, removeEdge, traverse (BFS with depth limit), getGraph. Overseer can now traverse decision chains for deeper analysis.
decisions: auto-link uses text overlap not AI (fast+free)

sessions.summary: v1.5.1 Impact Analysis Engine. Four analysis modes built on the Knowledge Graph: (1) Blast Radius -- traces downstream impact of changing a decision via directed edge traversal, (2) Contradiction Detection -- finds conflicting decisions via superseded edges + keyword opposition pairs, (3) Centrality Analysis -- ranks most foundational decisions by connection count, (4) Structural Holes -- identifies weak cross-project links. CLI: nexus impact blast/contradictions/centrality/holes. 39 CLI commands.
decisions: blast radius follows led_to+depends_on edges only
structural holes analyzes cross-project edge density

v3.1-alpha: Holes detection rewrite + Nexus MCP server v1. Holes now uses per-project union-find connected-components instead of cross-project pair matrix — flagged Nexus (2 clusters), Shadowrun (4 isolated), and general (14 clusters) as fragmented; Firewall-Godot and Resonance Godot as healthy. MCP server in server/mcp/index.ts exposes 6 v1 tools (brief, get_plan, check_guard, record_decision, push_thought, pop_thought) as a thin stdio adapter over the existing Express HTTP API. nexus mcp CLI subcommand prints Claude Code config snippet (claude mcp add or ~/.claude.json or .mcp.json). decisions: MCP server is a thin stdio ADAPTER over the existing HTTP API, not a parallel implementation — single source of truth, easy to extend
v1 ships 6 tools to validate the pattern; expansion to 10+ tools in follow-on work

Holes detection rewrite: per-project union-find on intra-project edges replaces noisy cross-project pair matrix; surfaces real fragmentation signal
CLI nexus mcp subcommand prints config for all 3 install paths (claude mcp add, ~/.claude.json, .mcp.json) so users can pick whichever fits their setup
Smoke test in server/mcp/smoke-test.mjs spawns the adapter as subprocess and runs full MCP protocol round-trips — protects against future regressions

ledger.decision: Noosphere uses Hebbian learning instead of backpropagation for agent neural networks
ledger.context: Biologically plausible (brains don't do backprop), runs entirely in GLSL compute shaders without needing PyTorch/libtorch, produces weirder and more emergent behavior than gradient-trained nets, no backward pass means massive parallelism
alternatives: Option A: PyTorch via libtorch GDExtension — rejected for build complexity and less interesting dynamics. Option B: Evolutionary strategies — considered as alternative
keeps as fallback. Option C: Neuroevolution (NEAT) — too slow for real-time learning.

ledger.decision: Noosphere uses Godot 4.6.1 compute shaders via RenderingDevice API instead of raw CUDA
ledger.context: Cross-platform Vulkan, built-in renderer, UI, audio, scene tree, and input handling — we only need to write the GLSL. 8GB VRAM on RTX 3070 Ti is far more than the ~14MB compute budget, so rendering and compute coexist cleanly. Hot-reload potential for shaders. No external build pipeline.
alternatives: Option A: Raw CUDA + custom renderer — rejected
months of infrastructure work. Option B: Python + CuPy + pygame — rejected
too slow for real-time rendering of field. Option C: Unity with compute shaders — possible but Godot 4.6.1 already on machine and lighter weight.

ledger.decision: Noosphere tensor field: 64x64x32 grid with 16 channels, PDE solver via wave equation + diffusion + damping
ledger.context: Field is the communication substrate and the game's narrator. Channel layout: 0-3 semantic, 4-5 emotion, 6-7 causal, 8-9 resources, 10-11 social, 12-13 player, 14-15 memory. 8MB VRAM double-buffered for Verlet integration. Workgroup (8,8,8) dispatched over full volume.
alternatives: Option A: Smaller 32^3 field — insufficient spatial detail. Option B: Larger 128^3 — 8x memory
not needed for target 60 agents. Option C: 2D field — rejected
loses volumetric dynamics that make emergent standing waves interesting.

ledger.decision: Noosphere phased development: PULSE -> SPARK -> MURMUR -> GAZE -> VILLAGE -> SOUL
ledger.context: Each phase proves a specific technical risk. PULSE = GPU pipeline + field works. SPARK = agents can learn. MURMUR = emergent communication exists. GAZE = player model feeds back. alternatives: Option A: Build everything in parallel — high risk of compounding failures. Option B: Skip straight to full sim — wastes effort if foundational pieces don't work. Option C: Research prototype in Python first — faster iteration but would need to port everything.
